success_criteria.description: All five districts are in use and every precinct is assigned.
All five districts have roughly equal population (within 10%).
Ken Party incumbents are protected — at least 3 districts won by a margin of 15 points or more.
Ryu Party incumbents are protected — at least 2 districts won by a margin of 15 points or more.
The map follows natural geographic lines — all districts are reasonably compact (≥ 35%).

character.name: You
character.motivation: Both parties have agreed — privately — that the current map is too competitive. Too many incumbents lost their seats last cycle. You've been hired by both sides to draw a new map where each party keeps what it has. The voters don't need to know the details.

intro_slides.heading: The Deal
intro_slides.body:
It doesn't happen at a press conference. It happens in a conference room, with lawyers from both parties, a map, and a handshake.

The current map has too many swing districts. Too many incumbents are at risk every two years. The solution, both sides agree, is simple: draw lines that protect everyone.

intro_slides.heading: Safe Seats for Everyone
intro_slides.body:
The goal isn't to help one party win more seats. It's to make sure neither party loses the seats they already have.

Concentrate Ken voters into Ken districts. Concentrate Ryu voters into Ryu districts. Eliminate the middle ground. Make every race a foregone conclusion.

intro_slides.heading: What the Voters Lose
intro_slides.body:
A safe seat means your representative doesn't need to worry about losing. They only need to survive their own primary — which means appealing to their party's base, not to the median voter.

Competitive elections create accountability. This map is designed to eliminate them. Notice how many races are decided before anyone votes.

narrative.objective: Draw a map that protects incumbents of both parties — Ken Party wins at least 3 safe seats (margin ≥ 15 points), Ryu Party wins at least 2 safe seats (margin ≥ 15 points).
